deck.title: Word Knowledge — Root Words
deck.description: Latin and Greek roots that unlock dozens of ASVAB vocabulary words.

cards.explanation: Examples: biology (study of life), biography (written account of a life), antibiotic (against living microbes).
Examples: geology (study of the earth), geography (description of the earth), geothermal (heat from the earth).
Examples: telephone (distant sound), symphony (sounds together), cacophony (harsh sound).
Examples: benevolent (well-wishing), beneficial (producing good), benefactor (one who does good).
Examples: malevolent (wishing harm), malign (to speak evil of), malady (a bad condition or illness).
Examples: audible (able to be heard), auditorium (place for hearing), audition (a hearing or trial).
Examples: dictate (to speak orders), verdict (a spoken truth), edict (an official statement).
Examples: portable (able to be carried), transport (carry across), deport (to carry away or expel).
Examples: inspect (look into), spectator (one who watches), circumspect (looking around, cautious).
Examples: visible (able to be seen), evident (clearly seen), envision (to see in the mind).
Examples: inscribe (to write into), manuscript (handwritten document), prescribe (to write an order for).
Examples: credible (believable), incredulous (unbelieving), credentials (proof that earns trust). Test trap: incredulous = unwilling to believe (the listener); incredible = unbelievable (the thing).
Examples: eject (throw out), interject (throw between), trajectory (path of something thrown).
Examples: transmit (send across), emissary (one sent on a mission), remit (to send back).
Examples: chronological (arranged by time), anachronism (out of its time), synchronize (occur at the same time).
Examples: fragment (a broken piece), fracture (a break), fraction (a broken-off part of a whole), refract (to break light's path), infraction (a breaking of rules).
Examples: metamorphosis (change of form), amorphous (without shape), anthropomorphic (having human form).
Examples: anthropology (study of humans), misanthrope (one who hates people), philanthropy (love of mankind).
Examples: fidelity (faithfulness), confidant (trusted friend), perfidy (breaking of faith, treachery).
Examples: diagnosis (knowing through symptoms), prognosis (foreknowledge of an outcome), agnostic (one without knowledge of the divine).
